functions.name: LoadLibraryExA
functions.signature: HMODULE LoadLibraryExA(LPCSTR lpLibFileName, HANDLE hFile, DWORD dwFlags);
functions.description: Load an ANSI-path module with explicit mapping and dependency-search behavior.
functions.longDescription: Load an ANSI-path module with explicit mapping and dependency-search behavior.

parameters.name: libraryPath
parameters.description: ANSI DLL path; prefer an absolute path with search flags.

parameters.name: flags
parameters.description: LoadLibraryEx flags. Use the advanced picker to combine compatible constants.

pickerConfig.title: Select LoadLibraryExA flags
pickerConfig.subtitle: Combine compatible mapping and search-policy flags.

sections.label: Flags
sections.description: Select one or more documented values.

items.label: DONT_RESOLVE_DLL_REFERENCES
items.description: Legacy compatibility mode: do not call DllMain and do not resolve dependencies. Prefer resource mapping flags for resource-only access.

items.label: LOAD_IGNORE_CODE_AUTHZ_LEVEL
items.description: Skip AppLocker and Software Restriction Policy checks for the loaded DLL only. Reserved for specialized installer scenarios.

items.label: LOAD_LIBRARY_AS_DATAFILE
items.description: Map the module as a data file for resource access. GetProcAddress cannot be used on a pure data-file mapping.

items.label: LOAD_LIBRARY_AS_DATAFILE_EXCLUSIVE
items.description: Map as a data file while preventing concurrent writes to the DLL file.

items.label: LOAD_LIBRARY_AS_IMAGE_RESOURCE
items.description: Map as an image resource without normal executable initialization.

items.label: LOAD_LIBRARY_SEARCH_DLL_LOAD_DIR
items.description: Search the loaded DLL directory for dependencies. The DLL path must be fully qualified.

items.label: LOAD_LIBRARY_SEARCH_APPLICATION_DIR
items.description: Search the application directory.

items.label: LOAD_LIBRARY_SEARCH_USER_DIRS
items.description: Search directories added with AddDllDirectory or SetDllDirectory.

items.label: LOAD_LIBRARY_SEARCH_SYSTEM32
items.description: Search System32.

items.label: LOAD_LIBRARY_SEARCH_DEFAULT_DIRS
items.description: Use the recommended application, user and System32 directory set.

items.label: LOAD_WITH_ALTERED_SEARCH_PATH
items.description: Use the alternate dependency search strategy. Use an absolute DLL path.

items.label: LOAD_LIBRARY_REQUIRE_SIGNED_TARGET
items.description: Require a valid digital signature for the binary image at load time.

items.label: LOAD_LIBRARY_SAFE_CURRENT_DIRS
items.description: Allow current-directory executable DLL loading only under directories in the safe load list.

functions.longDescription: Decrement a loaded-module reference count or release a data/image-resource mapping.

functions.name: FreeLibraryAndExitThread
functions.description: Atomically decrement a DLL reference count and terminate the calling thread.
functions.longDescription: Atomically decrement a DLL reference count and terminate the calling thread.

functions.name: GetProcAddress
functions.description: Retrieve an exported function or variable address. Select either an ANSI export name or an ordinal lookup from the same GetProcAddress API.
functions.longDescription: GetProcAddress is a single Win32 API. The lpProcName argument can identify an export by its exact ANSI name or by an ordinal encoded with MAKEINTRESOURCEA. Use the lookup-mode selector to enable only the relevant parameter.

parameters.name: module
parameters.description: Loaded DLL module returned by LoadLibrary* or obtained from an existing module handle.

parameters.name: lookupMode
parameters.description: Choose whether lpProcName is rendered from an ANSI export name or an ordinal.

options.description: Resolve by exact ANSI exported symbol name.
Resolve by MAKEINTRESOURCEA(ordinal).

pickerConfig.title: Select GetProcAddress lookup mode
pickerConfig.subtitle: GetProcAddress is one Win32 API. Select whether lpProcName contains an exported ANSI name or an ordinal encoded with MAKEINTRESOURCEA.

sections.label: GetProcAddress lpProcName forms
sections.description: The API accepts either an ANSI export name or an ordinal encoded in the low-order word.

items.label: By exported name
items.description: Resolve the exact ANSI export name, for example "Plugin_Run".

items.label: By ordinal
items.description: Resolve an ordinal from the DLL export table with MAKEINTRESOURCEA(ordinal).

parameters.name: procedureName
parameters.description: Exact ANSI exported symbol name. Enabled only for lookup by name.

parameters.name: ordinal
parameters.description: Export ordinal declared in the DLL export table. Enabled only for lookup by ordinal.

functions.name: Reference — DllMain loader-lock restrictions
functions.signature: /* DllMain must remain minimal. Defer complex initialization outside the loader lock. */
functions.insertText: /* DllMain must remain minimal. Do not call LoadLibrary or LoadLibraryEx from DllMain. Avoid synchronization with other threads, process creation, COM initialization, User32/GDI calls, and other loader-lock-sensitive work. Prefer explicit Initialize / Shutdown exports or lazy initialization. */

functions.name: Reference — LoadLibrary aliases and GetProcAddress variants
functions.signature: /* LoadLibrary and LoadLibraryEx are encoding-neutral aliases. GetProcAddress has one API and accepts either an ANSI export name or an ordinal. */
functions.insertText: /* LoadLibrary and LoadLibraryEx select A or W according to UNICODE. Prefer explicit W variants in Unicode Windows code. GetProcAddress has no W variant: exported PE symbol names are resolved with LPCSTR or with MAKEINTRESOURCEA(ordinal). Pair each successful LoadLibrary* call with FreeLibrary unless lifetime is intentionally pinned. */

functions.name: CMake Qt Widgets project with runtime DLL
functions.signature: CMake Qt Widgets project with runtime DLL
functions.insertText:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)

project(BLE_Sniffeur VERSION 0.1 LANGUAGES CXX)

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

find_package(Qt6 REQUIRED COMPONENTS Widgets)
qt_standard_project_setup()

qt_add_executable(BLE_Sniffeur
    main.cpp
    mainwindow.cpp
    mainwindow.h
    mainwindow.ui
)

target_sources(BLE_Sniffeur PRIVATE
    bledllloader.cpp
    bledllloader.h
    bledevice.h
)

target_link_libraries(BLE_Sniffeur PRIVATE Qt6::Widgets)

set(BLE_DLL_SOURCE "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/Scan_BLE_DLL.dll")
if(EXISTS "${BLE_DLL_SOURCE}")
    add_custom_command(TARGET BLE_Sniffeur POST_BUILD
        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E copy_if_different
            "${BLE_DLL_SOURCE}"
            "$<TARGET_FILE_DIR:BLE_Sniffeur>/Scan_BLE_DLL.dll"
        COMMENT "Copy Scan_BLE_DLL.dll beside the executable"
    )
else()
    message(WARNING "Scan_BLE_DLL.dll is missing from the project root")
endif()

set_target_properties(BLE_Sniffeur PROPERTIES
    MACOSX_BUNDLE TRUE
    WIN32_EXECUTABLE TRUE
)

include(GNUInstallDirs)
install(TARGETS BLE_Sniffeur
    BUNDLE DESTINATION .
    LIBRARY DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
    RUNTIME DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}
)
functions.description: Complete Qt Widgets CMakeLists.txt with sources, UI file, dynamic DLL copy, target properties, and installation.
functions.longDescription: A practical baseline matching a Qt Widgets application that loads a driver DLL dynamically with QLibrary. No import .lib is needed for symbols resolved exclusively at runtime.
functions.declaration: CMake Qt Widgets project with runtime DLL

functions.name: CMake Qt Widgets minimal project
functions.insertText:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)
project(MyQtApp VERSION 1.0 LANGUAGES CXX)

set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

find_package(Qt6 REQUIRED COMPONENTS Widgets)
qt_standard_project_setup()

qt_add_executable(MyQtApp
    main.cpp
    mainwindow.cpp
    mainwindow.h
    mainwindow.ui
)

target_link_libraries(MyQtApp PRIVATE Qt6::Widgets)

set_target_properties(MyQtApp PROPERTIES
    WIN32_EXECUTABLE TRUE
    MACOSX_BUNDLE TRUE
)
functions.description: Minimal modern Qt Widgets project with a .ui form.
functions.longDescription: Use qt_standard_project_setup() after find_package(Qt6) and before defining targets. Linking Qt6::Widgets also brings the Qt Core dependency transitively.

functions.name: CMake Qt optional modules
functions.insertText:
find_package(Qt6 REQUIRED COMPONENTS
    Core
    Widgets
    Network
    SerialPort
    Concurrent
)

find_package(Qt6 OPTIONAL_COMPONENTS
    Bluetooth
    SerialBus
    WebSockets
    WebChannel
)

target_link_libraries(MyQtApp PRIVATE
    Qt6::Core
    Qt6::Widgets
    Qt6::Network
    Qt6::SerialPort
    Qt6::Concurrent
)

if(TARGET Qt6::Bluetooth)
    target_link_libraries(MyQtApp PRIVATE Qt6::Bluetooth)
endif()
functions.description: Declare and link common optional Qt modules used by desktop and device applications.
functions.longDescription: Declare the Qt modules actually used by the application. Optional modules can be linked conditionally when the corresponding imported CMake target exists.

functions.name: CMake explicit AUTOMOC AUTOUIC AUTORCC alternative
functions.signature: CMake explicit AUTOMOC AUTOUIC AUTORCC alternative
functions.insertText:
set(CMAKE_AUTOMOC ON)
set(CMAKE_AUTOUIC ON)
set(CMAKE_AUTORCC ON)

add_executable(MyQtApp
    main.cpp
    mainwindow.cpp
    mainwindow.h
    mainwindow.ui
    resources.qrc
)

target_link_libraries(MyQtApp PRIVATE Qt6::Widgets)
functions.description: Enable Qt code generation explicitly when not using qt_standard_project_setup().
functions.longDescription: qt_standard_project_setup() normally enables common Qt CMake defaults. Explicit AUTOUIC, AUTOMOC, and AUTORCC settings remain useful when integrating Qt into an existing CMake project.
functions.declaration: CMake explicit AUTOMOC AUTOUIC AUTORCC alternative

functions.name: CMake copy runtime DLL beside executable
functions.signature: CMake copy runtime DLL beside executable
functions.insertText:
set(DRIVER_DLL "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/Scan_BLE_DLL.dll")

if(EXISTS "${DRIVER_DLL}")
    add_custom_command(TARGET MyQtApp POST_BUILD
        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E copy_if_different
            "${DRIVER_DLL}"
            "$<TARGET_FILE_DIR:MyQtApp>/Scan_BLE_DLL.dll"
        COMMENT "Copy runtime DLL beside MyQtApp"
    )
else()
    message(WARNING "Missing runtime DLL: ${DRIVER_DLL}")
endif()
functions.description: Copy a dynamically loaded DLL after each successful application build.
functions.longDescription: Use POST_BUILD with $<TARGET_FILE_DIR:...> so the runtime DLL is copied next to the produced executable for the active build configuration.
functions.declaration: CMake copy runtime DLL beside executable

functions.name: CMake cross-platform sidecar shared library
functions.insertText:
if(WIN32)
    set(DRIVER_FILE "Scan_BLE_DLL.dll")
elseif(APPLE)
    set(DRIVER_FILE "libScan_BLE_DLL.dylib")
else()
    set(DRIVER_FILE "libScan_BLE_DLL.so")
endif()

set(DRIVER_SOURCE "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/${DRIVER_FILE}")
if(EXISTS "${DRIVER_SOURCE}")
    add_custom_command(TARGET MyQtApp POST_BUILD
        COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E copy_if_different
            "${DRIVER_SOURCE}"
            "$<TARGET_FILE_DIR:MyQtApp>/${DRIVER_FILE}"
    )
endif()
functions.description: Copy a sidecar DLL, SO, or dylib next to the executable according to the target platform.
functions.longDescription: When the same application is built on several desktop platforms, deploy the conventionally named sidecar shared library for the current target OS.

functions.name: CMake install runtime driver DLL
functions.description: Install a sidecar runtime DLL with the application package.
functions.longDescription: POST_BUILD copying is convenient for development. Add an install(FILES) rule as well when producing an installation tree or package.

functions.name: CMake deploy Qt runtime dependencies
functions.insertText:
include(GNUInstallDirs)

install(TARGETS MyQtApp
    BUNDLE DESTINATION .
    RUNTIME DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}
)

qt_generate_deploy_app_script(
    TARGET MyQtApp
    OUTPUT_SCRIPT deploy_script
    NO_UNSUPPORTED_PLATFORM_ERROR
)

install(SCRIPT ${deploy_script})
functions.description: Generate an install-time Qt deployment script for a self-contained desktop application directory.
functions.longDescription: Qt provides qt_generate_deploy_app_script() for install-time collection of the Qt runtime dependencies required by an application. It is available from Qt 6.3.

functions.name: CMake configure Qt prefix path Windows
functions.signature: CMake configure Qt prefix path Windows
functions.description: Configure a Qt project when CMake cannot find the selected Qt kit automatically.
functions.longDescription: CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH should point to the selected Qt installation prefix containing the Qt CMake package files. Use the directory matching your compiler kit, such as MinGW or MSVC.
functions.declaration: CMake configure Qt prefix path Windows

functions.name: CMake configure build install workflow
functions.insertText:
cmake -S . -B build -G Ninja   -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release   -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX="${PWD}/install"

cmake --build build --parallel
cmake --install build
functions.description: Configure, compile, and install a Qt application from the command line.
functions.longDescription: A separate build directory keeps generated files outside the sources. Running cmake --install executes the install rules and any generated Qt deployment script.

Timer {
    interval: 1000
    running: true
    repeat: true
    triggeredOnStart: true
    onTriggered: backend.refresh()
